generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance CarThrottleController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Autonomous controller that dynamically adjusts throttle settings based on real-time monitoring data and safety thresholds"
]

instance SafetyViolationTrigger : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "System component that detects and triggers corrective actions when safety thresholds are violated"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance ForceSensorArray : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Array of sensors measuring dynamic forces acting on the car chassis during operation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ForceDataStream
]

instance TemperatureSensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Network of distributed temperature sensors monitoring engine, transmission, and structural components"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataStream
]

instance MotionAccelerometerCluster : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Cluster of accelerometers tracking 3D motion dynamics (X/Y/Z axes) of the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotionDataStream
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring environmental factors (wind, road surface conditions) affecting car dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance ForceDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ForceSensorArray
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol RabbitMQ
    DTDFVocab:dataFrequency Periodic
]

instance TemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensorNetwork
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol RabbitMQ
    DTDFVocab:dataFrequency Periodic
]

instance MotionDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotionAccelerometerCluster
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol RabbitMQ
    DTDFVocab:dataFrequency Periodic
]

instance EnvironmentalDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol RabbitMQ
    DTDFVocab:dataFrequency EventTriggered
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance CarDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Hybrid CAD-dynamic model combining static geometric representations with differential equation-based behavioral models for car motion analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DifferentialEquationSolver
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ParameterCalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance ForceResponseModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model describing force dynamics acting on car chassis components using differential equations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DifferentialEquationSolver
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateEstimationAlgorithm
]

instance ThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric and thermal model representing engine and transmission component temperature distribution"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalSimulationEngine
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model integrating simulation results with real-time data for performance optimization recommendations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControllerOptimizer
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FaultDetectionSystem
]

instance RealTimeDataBuffer : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing continuous sensor data streams for historical analysis and model validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ForceDataStream, TemperatureDataStream, MotionDataStream, EnvironmentalDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingPipeline
]

instance CalibrationParametersDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Centralized repository storing model parameter versions and calibration results for iterative refinement"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelVersionManager
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DifferentialEquationSolver : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation engine solving differential equations for dynamic car behavior modeling"
    DTDFVocab:enables CarDynamicsModel, ForceResponseModel
]

instance ParameterCalibrationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Iterative parameter optimization system adjusting model parameters to minimize discrepancies between simulated and real-world data"
    DTDFVocab:enables CarDynamicsModel, ThermalModel
]

instance StateEstimationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time state estimation system identifying anomalies and drift in sensor data for model validation"
    DTDFVocab:enables ForceResponseModel, PerformanceOptimizationModel
]

instance ControllerOptimizer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization framework recommending controller parameter adjustments to improve system performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:enables PerformanceOptimizationModel, CarThrottleController
]

instance FaultDetectionSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Pattern recognition system identifying operational faults via data-model correlations and real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:enables PerformanceOptimizationModel, SafetyMonitor
]

instance DataProcessingPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ETL pipeline processing raw sensor data for quality assurance, normalization, and feature extraction"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataBuffer, CalibrationParametersDatabase
]

instance ThermalSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine for thermal analysis of engine and transmission components"
    DTDFVocab:enables ThermalModel
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Core algorithmic component updating model parameters in real-time using feedback from physical twin sensors"
    DTDFVocab:enables ThermalModel, CarDynamicsModel
]

instance SafetyMonitor : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring system detecting deviations from safety thresholds and triggering corrective actions"
    DTDFVocab:enables SafetyViolationTrigger, PerformanceOptimizationModel
]

instance VisualizationDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization platform displaying real-time and simulated car performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:enables CarDynamicsVisualization, PerformanceMetricsDashboard
]

instance ModelVersionManager : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "System managing model evolution through iterative calibration cycles and version control"
    DTDFVocab:enables CarDynamicsModel, ThermalModel
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring service tracking operational metrics (forces, motion, temperature) and validating against expected behavior"
    DTDFVocab:provides ForcePerformanceInsight, TemperatureAnomalyDetection, MotionDeviationAlert
]

]

]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Autonomous adjustment service dynamically modifying physical twin parameters based on real-time monitoring"

]

instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adaptation service updating models with new data to maintain accuracy over time"
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterUpdateAction, ModelVersionMigration, FeedbackLoopClosure
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization service providing real-time and historical performance metrics visualization"

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  The **virtual-to-physical interaction** occurs via **RabbitMQ** as a command broker, transmitting control signals from the DT’s controller (hosted on a Raspberry Pi) to physical actuators (e.g., fans and heatbeds). Commands are generic (e.g., 'control the fan' or 'heatbed adjustments') and do not specify granular parameters like 'fan speed adjustments' or 'voltage changes' beyond experimental contexts. Feedback mechanisms are implied through **state estimation** and **monitoring** loops, where violations of operational thresholds trigger human or algorithmic interventions. No explicit authentication mechanisms are mentioned; however, a 'safe protocol' may imply basic security context. Command execution confirmation is not explicitly detailed beyond supervisory control, as the focus remains on model-driven optimisation and fault detection rather than real-time feedback loops.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system operates with **asynchronous temporal synchronization** between physical and digital components, where data collection from the Physical Twin (PT) occurs **over time** through sensor networks. The system does not specify explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements, but calibration algorithms dynamically update models based on real-time or near-real-time data streams to ensure alignment with PT behavior. The temporal granularity is not quantified, but the feedback loop (model updates → monitoring → intervention) implies iterative adjustments rather than fixed high-frequency cycles. The absence of explicit mention of real-time constraints or fine-grained time scales (e.g., milliseconds) limits precise specification beyond periodic data acquisition and model recalibration via closed-loop mechanisms."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system follows a **modular architecture pattern**, integrating three core components: **Data** (collected via sensors over time), **Models** (representing both cyber and physical aspects of the PT and its environment), and **Algorithms** (including fault detection, supervisory control, and optimization techniques). The system orchestrates these components through a **closed-loop feedback mechanism**: calibration algorithms dynamically update models based on real-time sensor data, which then informs monitors for safety and performance validation. Violations detected by monitors trigger interventions via algorithms or human operators, ensuring adaptive control of the Physical Twin. The **resource management** is implicit in the integration of these components, with the DT acting as a cohesive framework that leverages simulation, monitoring, and optimization technologies to enhance the Physical Twin’s value."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) in the provided context spans from **model development** to **integration with the Physical Twin (PT)**. Initially, models are created to represent specific aspects of the system, such as static attributes (e.g., CAD dimensions like wheel sizes, chassis shapes) or dynamic behaviors (e.g., differential equations for dynamics analysis). These models are not discarded upon deployment but are **calibrated and updated** using real-time data from sensors on the PT to refine their accuracy.

  During deployment, the DT incorporates **data streams** (collected via sensors), **models** (combining cyber and physical knowledge), and **algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) to enable continuous monitoring and predictive analysis. The lifecycle governance implicitly involves **transition management** between static and dynamic model representations, with calibration algorithms ensuring alignment between the DT and PT. While explicit governance frameworks are not detailed, the integration process emphasizes iterative updates to maintain relevance and predictive fidelity.

  No explicit lifecycle stages (e.g., design, deployment, operation) or governance structures (e.g., auditable change logs) are described in the documents, but the emphasis on **calibration and real-time adaptation** underscores the dynamic nature of the DT lifecycle.
 "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Model Fidelity Requirements**:
  - **Dynamic Models**: Must accurately represent PT behavior (e.g., differential equations) to predict outcomes under varying conditions.
  - **Calibration**: Models are updated via algorithms to align with real-world data (e.g., best parameter estimates from sensor inputs).

  **Validation/Verification Methods**:
  - **Simulation vs. PT Comparison**: DT simulations are validated by comparing predicted behavior (e.g., car dynamics) with actual PT performance.
  - **Iterative Refinement**: Calibration loops (e.g., algorithm-driven updates) ensure models stay aligned with PT evolution.

  **Uncertainty Sources**:
  - Model inaccuracies (e.g., idealized assumptions in differential equations).
  - Sensor noise or limitations (e.g., measurement errors in force/torque data).

  **Error Detection/Correction**:
  - **Monitors**: Algorithms detect deviations (e.g., safety violations) between simulated and real PT states.
  - **Automatic Adjustments**: Human or algorithmic interventions adjust PT behavior (e.g., actuators) or models (e.g., recalibration).

  **Quality Assurance**:
  - Implicitly tied to the DT’s closed-loop system: continuous validation of models/data via calibration and monitoring ensures reliability.
  - Not explicitly detailed, but critical for maintaining trust in DT predictions and optimizations.

  **Note**: The document emphasizes the DT’s role in preserving models post-deployment but does not elaborate on formal validation frameworks (e.g., ISO standards) or uncertainty quantification methods.
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The DT's technical connection relies on a **RabbitMQ server** for message brokering, deployed either **on-premises or in the cloud**, facilitating bidirectional communication between the DT and PT. The network infrastructure includes:
- **Communication Protocol**: RabbitMQ (AMQP-based messaging).
- **Network Topology**: Centralized server-based architecture with sensor-to-server and server-to-PT data/control flows.
- **Transmission Requirements**: Implied real-time or near-real-time synchronization (no latency metrics specified).
- **Reliability/Redundancy**: Not explicitly detailed, but RabbitMQ’s built-in message persistence and retry mechanisms are assumed for fault tolerance.
- **Security Protocols**: Not specified, but the system’s deployment context (e.g., industrial IoT) may imply standard encryption (TLS) for data-in-transit.
Sensors use **1-Wire bus** for low-power, multi-drop communication, while Raspberry Pi devices connect via **Wi-Fi/Bluetooth** for broader connectivity.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The DT hosting deployment supports **on-premises and cloud-based** configurations via RabbitMQ, with no specific computational/storage requirements detailed beyond the system’s modular architecture. The deployment model is flexible, accommodating distributed or centralized hosting. Availability/reliability is implied by the DT’s integration with the PT’s operational demands, though no uptime guarantees (e.g., 99.99%) or scalability metrics (e.g., Kubernetes clusters) are specified. The manuscript explicitly states that **detailed deployment specifics are outside its scope**, focusing instead on the DT’s core framework components.
"
]

    **Not in Document"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The provided Digital Twin (DT) framework integrates **calibration algorithms** to dynamically update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment using real-time sensor data from the Physical Twin (PT). This process ensures accurate parameter estimation, enabling reliable monitoring of system performance for safety and optimality. While the DT employs **monitoring systems** to detect deviations from safe operating conditions, the document does not specify explicit cybersecurity protocols, authentication mechanisms, threat mitigation strategies, fail-safe mechanisms, or adherence to industrial standards (e.g., IEC 62443 or ISO 26262). The focus remains on **model calibration and real-time validation** rather than broader security frameworks."
]
